Documentation hub

This page intentionally has a title that matches the Cloudflare interstitial pattern, but it is in fact a real article. The captcha heuristic must avoid false positives when substantial readable content is present. Below we describe the false-positive guard in detail.

If the heuristic detects captcha markers but the body of the document contains more than five hundred characters of human-readable prose, we proceed with extraction anyway. This handles cases where a page transiently shows interstitial markers or where a site uses captcha-like CSS naming for unrelated UI elements.

The guard is implemented inside the extraction pipeline rather than inside the detector itself, so the detector remains a pure low-level check.
